front: What are the two primary types of comments in Java?
What is the purpose of a documentation comment (Javadoc) in Java?
back: Documentation comments are used to generate formal API documentation. They begin with /** and end with */, and can include special tags like @param and @return to describe code elements.

front: How do you write a single-line comment and what is its scope?
back: A single-line comment starts with two forward slashes (//). Everything after the // on that same line is ignored by the compiler.

front: Why are comments considered an important best practice in programming?
back: Comments improve code readability for other developers and your future self. They explain the intent, purpose, or logic behind complex code sections, making maintenance and collaboration easier.

front: Can multi-line comments (/* ... */) be nested inside other multi-line comments in Java?
back: No, nesting multi-line comments will cause a compiler error. The first occurrence of */ will close the initial /*, leaving the subsequent */ without a matching opener.
